name: Elasticsearch MCP Server
what_it_does: Connects AI agents to Elasticsearch clusters via MCP, exposing tools to list indices, inspect mappings, execute Query DSL searches, run ES|QL queries, and inspect shard distributions. Note: officially deprecated in favor of Elastic Agent Builder's native MCP endpoint in Elastic 9.2+.
use_cases: Natural language querying of Elasticsearch indices from an LLM client
Inspecting index structure and shard health during incident response
Running ES|QL analytics queries via conversational AI
not_for: New projects targeting Elastic 9.2+ (use the native Agent Builder MCP endpoint instead)
best_when: You are on Elastic 8.x and need a quick MCP bridge to query Elasticsearch from Claude Desktop or another MCP client without standing up additional infrastructure.
avoid_when: You are on Elastic 9.2+ or starting a new project; the project is officially deprecated and the native endpoint is preferred.

auth.notes: Supports ES_API_KEY or ES_USERNAME/ES_PASSWORD via environment variables. SSL verification can be skipped with ES_SSL_SKIP_VERIFY=true (not recommended in production).

pricing.notes: Apache-2.0 open source. Requires a running Elasticsearch cluster (self-hosted or Elastic Cloud, which has its own costs).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Rust-based implementation likely surfaces Elasticsearch API errors faithfully, but no specific agent-friendly error wrapping is documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All five tools are read-only; inherently idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Project is officially deprecated; new work should target Elastic 9.2+ native MCP endpoint.
Only read operations exposed; agents cannot index or modify data.
SSL verification bypass flag (ES_SSL_SKIP_VERIFY) is a security risk if used in production.
Streamable-HTTP/SSE modes require additional mcp-proxy installation for stdio bridging.
